channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Hershey Reverses Chocolate Changes After Reese’s Cup Inventor’s Grandson Called Them Out Online
item.content:encoded:
Brad Reese, the grandson of the man who invented the Reese’s Cup, recently called out manufacturer Hershey’s for ditching milk chocolate and real peanut butter for cheaper ingredients in some of their candies. This public callout went viral, and now Hershey is committing to reversing its controversial candy changes in 2027.

Back in February, Reese posted a lengthy open letter on LinkedIn expressing frustration and disappointment over claims that Hershey’s was replacing milk chocolate with “compound coatings” and was swapping out peanut butter with “peanut-butter-style crème” across “multiple Reese’s products.” The news that the grandson of the guy who invented the Reese’s Cup was calling out Hershey’s for cheaping out quickly spread online. It also led to a lot of people claiming they had recently bought Reese’s Cups or other candies made by the company, and that they now tasted worse. Hershey’s responded a short time later, explaining that it never changed how it made Cups, its most popular product, but did admit that for other candies, it had tweaked formulas as cocoa prices increased. That answer didn’t stop people, and Reese, from complaining.

All of this ongoing negative publicity has now led to the chocolate maker promising to move all of its products back to real milk chocolate and peanut butter next year.

On April 1, as reported by The Associated Press via NBC, Hershey’s issued a statement about Chocolategate (as I’m calling it now) and once again confirmed it had never changed how it made Cups, but it had tweaked other candies, like mini-chocolate hearts for Valentine’s Day and eggs for Easter.

“Hershey is committed to making products consumers love, and that means continually reviewing our recipes to meet evolving tastes and preferences,” said Hershey’s in a statement.

The company confirmed that starting next year, it will move all of its candies back to “their classic milk chocolate and dark chocolate recipes.” The company also plans to transition to all-natural colors next year and is looking to “enhance” Kit-Kats to make them “creamier.” That last change scares me as Kit-Kats are already perfect, and if they fuck those up, I’ll be fuming.

Will this be enough for Brad Reese? Probably not. If you look at his LinkedIn page, you’ll see he’s been non-stop posting about Chocolategate. I’m not sure he’ll be happy until the candies are perfect, and that is unlikely to happen. Anyway, I’m going to go eat some Reese’s Cups.



https://kotaku.com/hershey-reverses-chocolate-changes-reeses-cup-inventors-grandson-called-out-2000683983
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 8 Things To Never Say To An Adult Lego Fan
item.content:encoded:
Adult fans of Lego, known online as AFOLs, have become a massive part of the toy company’s success over the last decade. Makes sense. Adults have more money than kids, are more likely to be suckered in by nostalgia, and are willing to spend a lot of cash on something that just sits on a shelf or hangs on a wall. These AFOLs, a term I hate using so I won’t use it anymore in this article, can vary in age, gender, and so forth, but there are some things that they all share. Some quirks and opinions that are mostly universal among these older Lego lovers, including myself. Here are eight things to never say to an adult Lego fan and why.

Legos are cool! 

Okay, look, I know this is going to sound pedantic and annoying, but please stop saying or writing “Legos.” Lego is a singular noun, never pluralized. If you mean Lego bricks or pieces, say that. If you mean Lego sets, well, just say that. There is never, ever, ever a reason to say “Legos.” It is weird to hear you say that or read a sentence that includes it. The Lego-loving nerds and adults in your life are judging you.

It’s so expensive

Yes. It is. There’s no getting around it. In fact, it’s getting more expensive, even. But there are some bright spots, like the incredibly affordable and amazing Game Boy set that came out last year. Still, there’s no denying it’s a pricey hobby. So maybe stop reminding us of the fact that these plastic bricks are so damn expensive every time you see a set. Trust us, we know.

Is this a toy for kids? 

Hey now, some of these sets are explicitly not designed for kids. Like the Star Wars Ultimate Collector Series of very pricey and massive sets based on ships and other gear from the franchise. There are also Lego flowers and art pieces that are meant to be built and then displayed, not played with by kids. So when I spend four hours building a Star Wars X-Wing ship, I’m not an adult playing with toys; I’m a hobbyist enjoying an adult activity. Right? RIGHT?!

Where do you put them all? 

Ask this question to most adult Lego lovers and they’ll sigh, look down at their feet, and mutter something like “I don’t know…” That’s because the more of these Lego sets you collect and build, the more room they quickly take up, and unless you own, like, a massive warehouse or extra home, you’ll quickly start running out of room. And it sucks! I’ve had to take apart and store old sets so I can display newer ones. If I ever win the lottery, you’ll know because I’ll stop posting here and start laying the foundation for my massive Lego workshop and museum.

Oh, I bought a Lego set once, and it contained a misprinted piece. Tossed it. 

WHAT? Please, if you ever find a misprinted minifig or other Lego piece, don’t trash it. Hold on to it. These can be valuable for collectors. These mistakes are very rare, making these pieces sought after by collectors. Sell a few misprints, and you’ll have enough money to buy any set you want.

I bet these are a pain to dust

You have no idea. I swear, Lego bricks are made out of a plastic that is designed to collect more dust than normal. My Star Wars ships are almost always covered in a thin coat of gray dust. Cleaning them can be a pain, as they often contain so many crevices, nooks, and crannies that getting them clean takes time. And if you drop it or push too hard, you could break it. Trust me when I say a lot of Lego collections are covered in dust.

The stickers seem so nice!

Lego stickers are wonderfully printed, and also annoy me and many other Lego builders and collectors, as they will one day peel off and make your set look worse. And trying to apply them can be a pain. I usually rely on my wife, who has smaller hands that are less shaky, to apply them; otherwise, I’ll screw it up and ruin the sticker. What Lego fans greatly prefer are printed pieces that feature custom art directly applied to the brick. These last a long time, look better, and won’t peel off one day and make you sad.

So this is like Mega Bloks? 

Fucking get out of here. Leave. Go away. I’m done.



https://kotaku.com/8-things-to-never-say-to-an-adult-lego-fan-2000683584
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Overwatch’s Hilarious April Fools’ Event Is Throwing Balance Out The Window Once More
item.content:encoded:
News

Overwatch’s Hilarious April Fools’ Event Is Throwing Balance Out The Window Once More

The ‘Underwatch’ mode turns a bunch of knobs and switches to create chaos

Overwatch

Every year Overwatch holds an April Fools’ Day event that adds a crazy new mode of chaotic balance changes and altered voice lines, and sticks googly eyes on the face of every hero. Blizzard still found time to add one this year even as it’s overhauling and rebooting the hero shooter with new updates, heroes, and lore drops. 

The “Underwatch” event is going up today, and some of the balance changes we can see in the announcement trailer include making Mei taller and probably buffing her like a tank, dialing up D.va’s projectiles into large, slow-moving homing missiles, breaking up Domina’s shield so it has a bunch of holes as if her tech is malfunctioning, and turning Cassidy’s finger-guns greeting into an actual gun. It also looks like Vendetta is getting a third-person lock-on view like you would see in a character action game. That’s fun.

The limited-time mode will be available from April 1 to 13. Just expect your Play of the Game highlights to include the googly eyes on your hero for a little bit regardless of whether you’re playing the mode. The full Underwatch patch notes are as follows:

Tank

D.Va

D.Va has realized micro missiles are too small, so why not giganto missiles?

Micro Missiles

  • Now fires two giant, slow-moving missiles that explode in a bigger area.

Domina has assigned Vishkar’s engineers to properly show off what their tech is capable of: funny shapes!

Barrier Array

  • Will randomly select one of many pre-determined Barrier Array configurations.
  • Barrier Panel Health increased by 50.
  • Cooldown reduced to 7 seconds.

For some reason, Doomfist took being usurped as head of Talon, thrown out of a window, and betrayed by former allies surprisingly personally.

  • Doomfist can see all active Talon members through walls.
  • Doomfist deals and receives 50% more damage from active Talon members.
  • Active Talon members: Vendetta, Emre, Sigma, Moira, Mauga, Freja

Rocket Punch

  • Charge can be held indefinitely.
  • Movement speed is no longer reduced when charge is held for long enough.

Phreak Phuel

Jagged Wall

  • No longer fires a wall-spawning projectile.
  • Instead, Hazard enhances himself for 5 seconds, greatly increasing his attack speed, reload speed, movement speed, cooldown speed, and jump speed.
  • Cooldown reduced to 8 seconds.

The infinite ingenuity of Junkertown presents: a shotgun full of knives.

Scattergun

  • Now shoots 7 knives instead of bullets.

Jagged Blade

  • Now throws a regular shotgun instead of a knife.
  • When impaling an enemy, the shotgun fires at the enemy.
  • After recalling, the shotgun fires at your crosshair when caught by Junker Queen.

Mauga is on fire! Everyone is on fire!

Overrun

  • Overrun can last forever.
  • Using Overrun for too long lights Mauga on fire.
  • While on fire from Overrun, colliding with enemies lights them on fire.

Cardiac Overdrive

  • Now makes Mauga’s chainguns shoot blobs of lava, which explode and instantly ignite enemies.

Mei has been moved to the Tank role to help minimize confusion when she somehow doesn’t die in combat.

  • Moved to Tank role and Bruiser subrole.
  • No longer has the Specialist subrole passive.
  • Shields increased from 0 to 200.
  • Mei’s scale has been increased by 25%.

Ice Wall

  • Ice Wall’s scale has been increased by 25%.

Yeet-haw, rodeo!

Energy Javelin

  • Now launches Orisa in the direction the Javelin is thrown.

Terra Surge

  • While charging Terra Surge, Orisa ascends into the sky and can move around.
  • Upon unleashing Terra Surge, Orisa slams down onto the ground below her, triggering its damage on impact.
  • No longer grants overhealth.
  • Maximum damage reduced to 400.

Introducing: Ramattra! The one-omnic show!

  • Health increased from 425 to 500.
  • Armor increased from 100 to 500.
  • Ramattra is now permanently anti-healed.

Ravenous Vortex

  • Ravenous Vortex now heals Ramattra for 250% of the damage dealt (bypassing anti-heal).

Annihilation

  • Annihilation now heals Ramattra for 100% of the damage dealt (bypassing anti-heal).
  • Annihilation applies anti-heal to enemies.

Back in his heyday, Reinhardt was named “pin king” at his local bowling joint.

Charge

  • Charge can pin multiple enemies at once.
  • Holding forwards or backwards speeds up or slows down Charge’s speed.
  • Charge’s duration increased from 3 seconds to 10 seconds.

Let him hook!

Chain Hook

  • Hook no longer pulls enemies.
  • Now slows, hacks, anti-heals, burns, and reveals the hooked enemy for 3 seconds.
  • Cooldown reduced from 6 seconds to 4.5 seconds.

Sigma’s going to show you his rock collection, and you’d better be nice!

Accretion

  • Now fires a shotgun volley of small rocks.
  • Has 2 charges.

Winston is currently unavailable due to a special reunion on Horizon Lunar Colony.

  • Winston has been disabled.
  • Seriously, you can’t play as Winston.

Wrecking Ball

“WHO ARE YOU CALLING ‘WRECKING SMALL’?!?”

Roll

  • Greatly increases Wrecking Ball’s size.
  • Wrecking Ball is unstoppable and takes 30% reduced damage during Roll, but moves significantly slower.

Grappling Craw

  • Retracting the Grappling Claw now launches Wrecking Ball in the direction he is moving.

As a child, Zarya would blow bubbles with her friends. As an adult, Zarya blows bubbles at her enemies.

Particle Cannon

  • Secondary fire now blows explosive bubbles.

Damage

Caw caw you cluckers.

Fan the Flames

  • While airborne, Anran’s secondary fire will push her backwards in the direction she is aiming.

Vermillion Revival

  • Anran can move around while dead before triggering Vermillion Revival.

Pull!

Dynamite

  • If Ashe shoots an airborne Dynamite, a new Dynamite will be launched upwards from the explosion.

We noticed Bastion was running a bit slow while in turret mode, so we replaced his gun with a large fan to hopefully speed him up.

Configuration: Assault

  • Now shoots large wind-blasts with limited range, knocking back enemies.
  • Bastion now moves faster while in this configuration instead of moving slower.

In the wild west, fingerguns are seen as a sign of hostility.

  • Cassidy fires a piercing bullet that inflicts bleeding whenever saying hello.

Shapes, flight, bouncy bombs, oh my!

Tri-Shot

  • Each successive shot increases the number of projectiles fired, creating new shapes for each volley.

Flight

  • Cancelling Flight launches Echo in the direction she is moving.

Sticky Bombs

  • Sticky Bombs now bounce off the environment up to 3 times before sticking.

We’ve evolved Emre’s combat so players can reach a higher skill ceiling with his kit.

  • Emre’s jump height has increased.
  • Emre’s gravity has decreased.
  • 200 of Emre’s health has been converted to shields.

Cyber Frag

  • Projectile physics have been changed.
  • Now sticks to enemies, dealing more damage when stuck.

Siphon Pistol

  • Projectile speed increased.
  • Can now headshot.
  • Lifesteal removed.
  • No longer changes Emre’s jump behavior.

Quick Melee

  • Now deals 60 damage.
  • Will lunge at nearby enemies.
  • Deals critical damage when hitting enemies in the back.

Override Protocol

  • Ultimate cost has been reduced by 50%.
  • Now only fires 2 heavy rounds.
  • Rate of fire has increased.
  • No longer grants flight.

With Emre hogging all the airspace, Freja’s taking a more grounded approach.

  • Freja’s regular movement is 70% slower.
  • Freja’s cooldowns are accelerated by 300% while on the ground.

Genji has been reworked to be our third-ever first-ever melee DPS.

Shuriken

  • Moved from his weapon to his Ultimate.
  • Shuriken projectile speed tripled.
  • Primary fire now continues firing when held down.
  • Secondary fire now fires 9 projectiles at a faster rate and in a wider arc.
  • Ultimate duration increased to 8 seconds

Energy Slash

  • New ability.
  • When activated, Genji’s Dragonblade will fire large energy projectiles with each slash for a short duration.
  • Only usable with Dragonblade equipped.
  • Has an 8-second cooldown.

Shuriken

  • Moved from his weapon to his Ultimate.
  • Shuriken projectile speed tripled.
  • Primary fire now continues firing when held down.
  • Secondary fire now fires 9 projectiles at a faster rate and in a wider arc.
  • Ultimate duration increased to 8 seconds.

The Yokai replaced Hanzo’s quiver with one they found in a warehouse as a prank.

Random Arrow

  • Replaces Sonic Arrow.
  • Has a 6 second cooldown.
  • Readies one of many possible arrows at random into Hanzo’s bow, which can be seen before firing.

After getting booted from Junkertown, Junkrat has founded New Trap City.

Steel Trap

  • Maximum number of active Steel Traps has been increased from 1 to 3.
  • No longer has a cooldown and instead costs 50 scrap to place.
  • Heroes drop 25 scrap upon death, which Junkrat can pick up.

Pharah knew she couldn’t compete with a beloved cat in a jetpack so she decided to ground herself as well.

Rocket Launcher

  • Has increased self-knockback.
  • The more Pharah knocks herself back with her Rocket Launcher, the faster she moves.
  • No longer deals damage to Pharah.

Reaper’s learned to play peak-a-boo!

Shadow Step

  • During Shadow Step, Reaper has 6 seconds to choose if he wants to teleport there or retreat back to his original position.
  • If he retreats, Shadow Step’s cooldown is reduced to 5 seconds.

She’s on that Tom Beansons caffeine kick.

  • While at 100 energy, Sojourn’s abilities consume 50 energy to become energized.

Power Slide

  • Energized Power Slide moves 100% faster and trips enemies Sojourn collides with.

Disruptor Shot

  • Energized Disruptor Shot leaves behind 2 smaller energy bursts as it travels.
  • The smaller energy bursts have a 2-meter radius and deal 60 damage per second.

Solder: 76’s gym routine: leg day, every day, 7 days a week.

Biotic Field

  • Soldier: 76 can deal massive damage to enemies if he places Biotic Field on them.

Sprint

  • Final Blows increase Soldier: 76’s Sprint bonus speed boost by 25%, up to 75% extra.
  • This bonus is lost on death.

For unspecified reasons, barrels have appeared on all maps. Please ignore them.

Disguise

  • Replaced Translocator.
  • Transforms Sombra into a random object.
  • Sombra can still move while disguised.
  • Doing any action removes Sombra’s disguise.

Symmetra’s Sentry Turrets are so clingy these days.

Sentry Turret

  • Sentry Turrets now automatically deploy around Symmetra and follow her around wherever she goes.
  • Number of charges increased to 4 from 3.
  • Cooldown reduced to 7.5 seconds.
  • Damage reduced from 30 to 15.

Hard Hard work work pays pays off off!!

  • A second Torbjorn is on top of Torbjorn, mimicking all of his actions.

Tracer’s chrono-accelerator is accelerating her chrono more than ever before.

Blink

  • Distance increased to 100 meters.

No one truly knows what Vendetta has gone through, especially not now that we moved the camera.

  • Vendetta’s camera is always in 3rd person, with her aim being totally separate from the player’s.
  • Vendetta will automatically lock on to nearby enemies, facing them no matter how the player moves.
  • Press Interact to lock on to the nearest enemy your crosshair is aiming at.

Palatine Fang

  • Overhead damage reduced by 40%.

Ooooh, buried treasure!

Burrow

  • When emerging from Burrow, a random pick-up is also dug out of the ground.
  • The pick-up can be collected by any ally.

You think you’re so good at aiming? Prove it.

Take Aim

  • Can now charge up to 150%.
  • When charging above 100%, the charge rate is 80% slower and the scope zooms in even further.

Support

Now this is what we call a power nap.

Sleep Dart

  • Now puts Ana to sleep for up to 3 seconds.
  • When waking up, Ana will Nano Boost herself.
  • The Nano Boost’s duration will scale based on how long she was asleep.

Baptiste is looking rather dashing.

Exo Boots

  • Now charges up to 4 air-dashes.
  • Air-dashes are used by jumping in mid-air, launching Baptiste horizontally in the direction he is moving.
  • Air dash charges do not decay over time.

Why stop at ‘shield bash’ when you can also ‘shield surf’?

Shield Bash

  • After Shield Bashing, Brigitte begins surfing on another shield for 3 seconds, increasing her movement speed and jump height.
  • Shield Bash’s cooldown begins once the shield surfing ends.
  • Cooldown reduced to 3 seconds.

Now that’s what we call a solar flare!

Healing Pylon

  • No longer passively heals nearby allies.
  • Shooting the pylon with Solar Rifle’s primary fire reflects the projectile to all enemies in range.
  • Solar Rifle’s secondary fire can heal the pylon.
  • Shooting the pylon with Solar Rifle’s secondary fire reflects the beam to all damaged allies in range.
  • Build time reduced to 0.5 seconds.
  • Illari can throw the Pylon further.
  • Cooldown is always 7 seconds.

Meow!!! Meow mrOW mroW MROAR ROARRRR…

Lifeline

  • Comes pre-attached with a Wrecking Ball that can be used to slam into enemies.
  • Ending Lifeline launches the ball at high speeds in the direction it is moving.
  • Now has a 12-second duration.
  • Cooldown increased to 6 seconds.

Having grown up on Mars, Juno never got to experience the joy of a trampoline… until now.

Hyper Ring

  • Hyper Ring is now deployed sideways.
  • Allies within Hyper Ring can jump higher. Wheeee!

Eat your heart out, Mizuki.

Wall Climb

  • Kiriko can move along walls horizontally while wall climbing.
  • After wall-climbing, Kiriko can re-grab the wall without needing to touch the ground.

Lifeweaver’s size was scaled down in a previous patch. We believe we did not go nearly far enough.

  • Lifeweaver’s scale has been reduced by 50%.
  • Health reduced from 200 to 75.
  • Shields reduced from 50 to 25.

Life Grip

  • Allies pulled by Life Grip get shrunk for 5 seconds after being pulled.

Petal Platform

  • Petal Platform’s scale has been reduced by 50%.
  • Health reduced from 300 to 150.

Tree of Life

  • Tree of Life’s scale has been reduced by 50%.
  • Health reduced from 1200 to 600.

Putting the hop in hip hop.

  • Successive jumps off of the floor increase Lucio’s jump height, up to 5 times.
  • Failing to chain jumps together resets Lucio’s jump height back to normal.
  • When at maximum jump height, Lucio can land on an enemy’s head, dealing damage and bouncing him back up.

The team won’t need healing if the enemy dies first.

Guardian Angel

  • Can now target enemies.

Resurrect

  • Now only targets enemies.
  • Using “Resurrect” on an enemy deals damage while casting, ending in an explosion of damage when finished.
  • Mercy heals for 50% of the damage dealt with Resurrect.
  • Cooldown reduced to 15 seconds.

We have fixed an issue with Mizuki where he was not wearing his iconic 3-hat-stack.

Healing Kasa

  • Healing Kasa has 3 charges.
  • Mizuki now wears a hat for each charge of Healing Kasa available.
  • Healing Kasa no longer returns to Mizuki.
  • While Mizuki has 1 or more charges of Healing Kasa, he cannot be headshot.

Katashiro Return

  • Mizuki can teleport to his previous location 3 times per use.

The orbs are orbing.

Biotic Orb

  • Now requires targeting an ally to cast.
  • The Biotic Orb will attach itself to the targeted ally, moving around with them.
  • Damage orb’s range increased from 4 meters to 8 meters.
  • Healing orb’s range increased from 5 meters to 10 meters.

Wuyang’s Guardian Wave has been properly adjusted to show just how washed the enemy team is.

Guardian Wave

  • Wuyang can charge up Guardian Wave, empowering its size, damage, healing, and knockback.

Spin to win!

Orb of Destruction

  • Secondary fire now has a 6-second cooldown.
  • While charging secondary fire, orbs begin circling around Zenyatta, dealing damage and knocking back nearby enemies for up to 8 seconds.
  • When releasing secondary fire, the orbs are shot out all around Zenyatta rather than all straight-forwards.
  • Secondary fire’s damage and knockback increase with each orb charged.
  • Zenyatta moves 30% faster while charging Orb of Destruction.
  • Zenyatta gains 15 overhealth with each stored orb.

Outside of the April Fools’ event Blizzard is teasing the game’s next hero, who will be joining the roster in two weeks when the next season starts. All we’ve seen so far is an illustration, but fans have connected the dots and think she may be a character from one of the game’s novels.

🕹️ Level up your inbox

Don’t miss the latest reviews, news and tips. Sign up for our free newsletter.
